$schema_note: F2-01 event-bus taxonomy. FUTURE-STATE (constraint C-07): describes the daemon event bus F2-02..F2-04 will build; no live mechanism has changed. Supersedes the NATIVE-1-13 migration tranches (closed-as-superseded at F2-M per DEC-070). Tranche A = advisory (fail OPEN on daemon death). Tranche B = deny-capable (fail CLOSED on daemon death, C-06). Consumer names are current hook FILES whose LOGIC becomes a resident daemon handler; the file itself shrinks to the shared ping shim.

fail_policy_classes.advisory-fail-open: Tranche A. Ping is best-effort via event.emit; a dead/unreachable daemon means the advisory simply does not fire (worst case: a lost banner or an unwritten capture row). Shim returns rc=0 on any RPC miss.
fail_policy_classes.deny-capable-fail-closed: Tranche B. Verdict is synchronous via event.verify. During the F2-04 shadow+rollback window the retained hook body is the fallback (still enforced locally). Post-cutover, an RPC miss/timeout MUST return DENY (shim rc=2) — a deny gate that silently fails open on daemon death is a security hole (C-06).

events.name: prompt.submitted
events.note: context-reset-monitor.py is NOT a consumer — it is keep-as-hook (identity-critical post-compaction re-injection, must run daemon-independently).

events.name: dispatch.pre.verify
events.note: routing-target-validator.py (TASK-074, 2026-07-16, owner-approved F2-04 constraint) is a mandatory member of this deny-capable set. Order preserved cheapest-first per gate_runner.py EVENT_CHAINS; routing-target-validator runs on the Task|TeamCreate subset only.

events.name: dispatch.pre.observe
events.note: lane-router and review-panel are flag-gated advisory adapters today (lane-router.enabled / review-panel.enabled); they remain advisory-emit only.

events.name: subagent.stop.verify
events.note: plan-validation-gate.py is deny-capable + fail-CLOSED by its own construction (scorer error = FAIL, never silent pass) — preserve that under the daemon path.

events.name: subagent.stop.observe
events.note: completion-capture.py MUST run even when subagent.stop.verify denies — under the current multiplexer it is deliberately ordered FIRST so a REVISE deny never skips telemetry (gate_runner.py Finding #6). The bus MUST preserve this: observe handlers fire independently of the verify verdict, never short-circuited by it.

events.name: read.completed
events.note: read-injection-scanner is advisory (adds warning context) not deny-capable — its DATA-not-instructions verdict is surfaced, never a block.

events.name: session.start
events.note: inject-invariants.sh and ensure-daemon.sh are NOT consumers — both keep-as-hook (identity-critical injection / daemon bootstrap). health-banner.sh is invoked today by memory-health-check.sh; it rides the same session.start resident handler.

events.name: doc.written
events.note: Forward event for F2-07. A path-filtered specialization of write.post.observe firing only on governed-corpus doc paths. Watcher ALWAYS flags; AUTO-UPDATE only within the FDEC-6 authority boundary (mechanical fixes). Watcher writes are themselves watched (C-04) — the watcher's own doc.written re-enters the bus.
